content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 16, 1991


Judge Rosemary M. Dunbar
102 Santa Ana Ave.
Long Beach, CA 90803

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-91-404

Dear Judge Dunbar:

You have requested advice concerning your reporting
obligations under the conflict of interest disclosure provisions
of the Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTION

As a retired judge, are you required to continue filing
Statements of Economic Interests (Form 721) with each court to
which you receive a temporary assignment?

CONCLUSION

Because you have not filed a leaving office statement for the
Superior Court of Los Angeles County and you expect to serve 30
days in a 12-month period, you will continue filing annual
statements of economic interests. Your statement will be filed
with the clerk of the court to which you are assigned at the time
you file your statement. If you are not assigned to a specific
court at the time you file your statement, you will file your
statement with the clerk of the court of your last assignment.

When your assignment ends and you do not anticipate receiving
a new assignment you must file a leaving office statement within
30 days of the termination of your assignment.


___________________________
1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Judge Rosemary Dunbar
page 2


FACTS

You recently retired from the Los Angeles Superior Court.
You have not filed a leaving office statement with the clerk of
that court. As a retired judge, you will be receiving temporary
assignments to various courts.

ANALYSIS

Section 87200, et seq., requires that judges file statements
of economic interests (Form 721). However, regulation 18724^2/
provides that retired judges are not required to file statements
of economic interests pursuant to Government Code Section 87200,
et seq., if they serve or expect to serve less than 30 days or
more in any 12-month period.

Once your assignment ends and you do not receive or
anticipate receiving a new assignment, you must file a leaving
office statement within 30 days of leaving your last assignment.
The leaving office statement will be filed with the clerk of the
court of your last assignment. (Cooper Advice Letter, No. 91-030.)

If you have any questions or need further assistance, please
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

by: Colleen McGee
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division


2/ Copies of Commission regulations and opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a retired judge must continue filing Statements of Economic Interests (Form 721) with courts for temporary assignments after retiring from the Superior Court.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A retired judge who has not filed a leaving office statement and expects to serve 30+ days in a 12-month period must continue filing annual statements of economic interests with the clerk of the court of assignment, and must file a leaving office statement within 30 days when assignments end.
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, ocr; LLM: Document has OCR quality issues (OCE, seo., ceneral, etc.) but all major sections clearly identifiable. Question and Conclusion sections are clearly marked and verbatim extraction is reliable. Analysis section supports the conclusion.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: As a retired judge, are you required to continue filing Statements of Economic Interests (Form 721) with each court to which you receive a temporary assignment?

CONCLUSION: Because you have not filed a leaving office statement for the superior Court of Los Angeles county and you expect to serve 30 days in a 12-month period, you will continue filing annual statements of economic interests. Your statement will be filed with the clerk of the court to which you are assigned at the time you file your statement. If you are not assigned to a specific court at the time you file your statement, you will file your statement with the clerk of the court of your last assignment. When your assignment ends and you do not anticipate receiving a new assignment you must file a leaving office statement within 30 days of the termination of your assignment.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises Judge Rosemary M. Dunbar that as a retired judge receiving temporary court assignments and expecting to serve at least 30 days in a 12-month period, she must continue filing annual Statements of Economic Interests with the appropriate court clerk and file a leaving office statement within 30 days when her assignments terminate.
